claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're enquiring about.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you, and I'm happy to help with your Child Benefit enquiry for Alex.

Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you!

**Are you responsible for Alex's care and upbringing?** This could include things like paying for food, clothes, and other daily needs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for everything - food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for clarifying that.

Now I just need to check a couple more things:

**What is your immigration or residency status?** Specifically:
- Are you a British or Irish citizen, or do you have the right to live in the UK (such as settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another valid immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen and I live here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question:

**Is Alex in full-time education or registered with a careers service, or if not, is Alex under 16 years old?**

(Since you mentioned Alex is 8, I expect this is straightforward, but I just want to confirm Alex's situation.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is only 8, so still in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- Alex is 8 years old and lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex's care (food, clothes, school things, etc.)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is in full-time education (primary school)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **You are a British citizen living in the UK** – this meets the residency requirement

✅ **Alex is under 16 years old** – children under 16 qualify

✅ **Alex lives with you** – this establishes your responsibility

✅ **You are responsible for Alex's care** – paying for food, clothes, and other needs confirms this

✅ **No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex** – only one person can claim per child

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. If you haven't already, you'll need to apply through HMRC. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old). The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK and therefore has the right to reside. Alex lives with the claimant, who is responsible for their care and upbringing (paying for food, clothes, school things). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is under 16 years old (8 years old). The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK and therefore has the right to reside. Alex lives with the claimant, who is responsible for their care and upbringing (paying for food, clothes, school things). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.
